articles.title: Gränges AB annual general meeting approves SEK 3.40 dividend and adopts LTI 2026 programme
articles.summary: Gränges AB's annual general meeting on 12 May 2026 approved a dividend of SEK 3.40 per share and adopted the LTI 2026 long-term incentive programme comprising up to 1,000,000 warrants and 500,000 restricted share units for senior managers and key individuals, targeting financial value creation and sustainability over approximately three years.
articles.novelis_angle: The LTI programme signals Gränges's commitment to retaining management talent as it competes for automotive heat-exchanger strip demand—a segment where Novelis also supplies aluminium rolled products for EV thermal management applications.

articles.title: RecAL targets smarter aluminium recycling across Europe
articles.summary: The EU Horizon Europe-funded RecAL project, led by the Austrian Institute of Technology with 19 partners from 9 European countries, is developing 14 circular amplification technologies and a digital RecAL Hub cockpit for aluminium recyclate traceability and sorting, targeting annual cost savings of at least €170 million across the European aluminium recycling sector.
articles.novelis_angle: The RecAL Hub's recyclate-tracking infrastructure could complement Novelis's closed-loop programmes in Europe by improving sorting yields on post-consumer scrap, supporting progress toward the 3x30 recycled-content targets.

articles.title: LME aluminium cash offer climbs to $3,729.5/t, near four-year high, on Middle East supply disruptions
articles.summary: The LME aluminium cash offer climbed to $3,729.5/t on 13 May 2026—the highest level since March 2022—driven by Middle East supply disruptions affecting approximately 9% of global aluminium output and 25% of non-Chinese supply, with forward curve compression signalling strong physical demand from buyers seeking spot metal.
